name: Cook Islands

colors.symbolism: the Pacific Ocean and the Blue Ensign tradition; ties to the United Kingdom
the 15 stars representing the 15 islands; the white of the Union Jack
the red of the Union Jack — historic ties to the British Crown

description: A Blue Ensign with the Union Jack in the upper-hoist canton and, in the fly, fifteen white five-pointed stars arranged in a circle.
history: Adopted on 4 August 1979, replacing a green ensign with two interleaved circles of stars used from 1973. The Cook Islands are a self-governing nation in free association with New Zealand; the flag's Blue Ensign form, complete with Union Jack canton, reflects this constitutional status within the Commonwealth.

features.symbolism: the blue field of the Blue Ensign — the surrounding Pacific Ocean and ties to the United Kingdom and the Commonwealth
ties to the United Kingdom and the Commonwealth; the Cook Islands are in free association with New Zealand, which itself remains a Commonwealth realm

features.role: the fifteen islands of the Cook Islands
features.symbolism: Fifteen white five-pointed stars arranged in a circle, representing the fifteen islands of the Cook Islands group; the circle implies their unity.
